content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 16, 1996

Mr. Kevin Rehm, President
El Segundo Firefighter's Assn.
I.A.F.F. Local 3682
P.O. Box 55
El Segundo, CA 90245

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-96-222

Dear Mr. Rehm:

You have requested advice on behalf of the El Segundo
Firefighter's Association ("Association") regarding the campaign
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/    Because your
inquiry is general in nature, we will treat your letter as a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

Is the Association required to register and file campaign
disclosure reports as a recipient committee?

CONCLUSION

The Association does not qualify as a committee under the Act
and does not have any campaign reporting responsibilities.

___________________________

1/    Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

2/    Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

/...

Mr. Kevin Rehm
Our File No. I-96-222
Page Two

FACTS

The activities of the Association consist of placing
newspaper ads on fire engine staffing requirements as provided by
the National Fire Protection Association, OSHA, etc.; educating
the public on fire engine staffing on Access Cable Television; and
soliciting input from the community by establishing a list of
supporters of the Firefighter's Association. The Association does
not support any candidate, officeholder or measure.

ANALYSIS

The Association would be required to file campaign statements
if it qualifies as a "committee" within the meaning of the Act.
A group is a "committee" if it receives contributions totalling
$1,000 or more in a calendar year. (Section 82013(a)). A
"contribution" is defined to include any payment, unless it is
clear from surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes. (Section 82015.) A payment is made for
"political purposes" if it is used to support or oppose specific
state or local candidates or ballot measures. (Regulation 18215.)

Based upon the information you provided, the Association does
not qualify as a committee.

Please call me at (916) 322-5660 if you have additional
questions.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
